findings.description: The entire memorandum content (PAT McCRORY/VAN DER VAART/DAVIS header, MEMORANDUM heading, date, To/From/Subject, body text, and outline) is duplicated in the HTML. The full first-page memo content appears twice consecutively, separated by footer text and a color bar, before the History/Background section begins.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has a single instance of the memorandum. In the HTML, the PAT McCRORY header info → MEMORANDUM heading → May 13, 2016 → To/From/Subject → body paragraph → outline appears once, then after a footer block and color bar, the entire sequence (PAT McCRORY → MEMORANDUM → memo content → outline) repeats a second time before transitioning to I. History/Background.

findings.description: Footer text and a decorative color bar are inserted between the NC logo/letterhead and the main memorandum content, disrupting the header reading order. Additionally, footer blocks are inserted between the memo outline and the History/Background section.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the NC logo (top-left) and PAT McCRORY/VAN DER VAART/DAVIS info (top-right) appear together at the top of page 1. In the HTML, the NC logo appears first, followed by a large blank space, then the full page footer ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality…'), then a color bar, and only then the PAT McCRORY header info and MEMORANDUM content. A similar footer+colorbar block is also inserted between the outline and the I. History/Background section heading.

findings.description: Governor's surname capitalization altered. The PDF shows 'PAT McCRORY' (all uppercase after 'Mc'), but the HTML renders it as 'PAT McCrORY' with a lowercase 'r'.

A decorative page-bottom bar or background image from the PDF was converted into a standalone figure placed mid-content (between the TOC and Section I) with generic alt text 'Decorative page background'. While it is indeed decorative, it should ideally have an empty alt attribute (alt="") so assistive technology skips it, and its mid-content placement is unnecessary.
